questions.text: Cubic crystal system has the following unit cell dimensions:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The correct answer is a=b=c and α=β=γ=90°.The cubic crystal system is one of the seven crystal systems.It is characterized by having all three axes equal in length and all angles between the axes equal to 90°.This means that the crystal is symmetrical about three axes,and the atoms in the crystal are arranged in a cubic pattern

options.explanation: Option a=b≠c and α=β=γ=90° describes the tetragonal crystal system.

options.text: a ≠ b ≠ c and α = β = γ = 90°
options.explanation: a≠b≠c and α=β=γ=90° describes the orthorhombic crystal system.
α = β = 90° γ = 120° describes the hexagonal crystal system.

questions.text: The crystalline form of white Tin is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Gray tin is the stable form of tin at temperatures below 13.2 °C. It has a body-centered cubic crystal structure, with each atom surrounded by eight other atoms.
The crystalline form of white tin is tetragonal.Tin has two crystalline forms: white tin and gray tin.

The crystalline form of white tin is tetragonal.Tin has two crystalline forms: white tin and gray tin. White tin is the stable form of tin at temperatures above 13.2 °C.It has a tetragonal crystal structure, with each atom surrounded by four other atoms. Gray tin is the stable form of tin at temperatures below 13.2 °C.It has a body-centered cubic crystal structure,with each atom surrounded by eight other atoms. The tetragonal crystal structure of white tin is responsible for its brittleness.The atoms in the crystal are arranged in a regular pattern, but the pattern is not as strong as the pattern in the body-centered cubic crystal structure of gray tin.This makes white tin more likely to break when it is subjected to stress.
One substance can have only one structure at one time in one form/physical state.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct.The crystalline form of white tin is tetragonal.Tin has two crystalline forms: white tin and gray tin.White tin is the stable form of tin at temperatures above 13.2 °C.It has a tetragonal crystal structure, with each atom surrounded by four other atoms.Gray tin is the stable form of tin at temperatures below 13.2 °C.It has a body-centered cubic crystal structure,with each atom surrounded by eight other atoms.The tetragonal crystal structure of white tin is responsible for its brittleness.The atoms in the crystal are arranged in a regular pattern,but the pattern is not as strong as the pattern in the body-centered cubic crystal structure of gray tin.This makes white tin more likely to break when it is subjected to stress.

questions.text: Which is incorrect about structure of iodine:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Face-centered cubic is a type of crystal lattice structure where atoms are located at the corners and centers of the faces of the unit cell. This option doesn't provide any information that is inherently incorrect about the structure of iodine.

Iodine's three-dimensional crystal structure allows it to possess a reflective quality, known as "metallic luster," due to its ability to reflect most of the light that falls upon it. While iodine is a non-metal, this property is attributed to its crystal structure rather than its actual metallic nature.

This option suggests that the bond length between iodine atoms in the gaseous state is greater than the bond length in the solid state. This is incorrect. The bond length in the solid state is greater due to intermolecular forces and lattice structures, which cause the iodine molecules to be spaced farther apart.

This option suggests that the bond length between iodine atoms in the gaseous state is smaller than the bond length in the solid state. This is the accurate statement. Iodine molecules in the gaseous state have shorter bond lengths compared to the solid state due to the weaker van der Waals forces in the gas phase.

questions.text: In NH3, dominant Van der Waal's force among following is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: London dispersion forces, also known as van der Waals forces, are temporary attractive forces that arise due to temporary fluctuations in electron distribution. While these forces are present in all molecules, they tend to be weaker in NH₃ compared to other intermolecular forces like dipole-dipole interactions and hydrogen bonding. Incorrect

This interaction refers to the interaction between a polar molecule and a nonpolar molecule, where the polar molecule induces a temporary dipole in the nonpolar molecule. In the case of NH₃, where the molecule itself is polar, this interaction is less relevant. Incorrect

Dipole-dipole forces occur between polar molecules where the positive end of one molecule interacts with the negative end of another molecule. NH₃ has a permanent dipole moment due to the difference in electronegativity between nitrogen and hydrogen, so dipole-dipole forces are indeed present. But these forces are not more dominant than hydrogen bonding. Incorrect

Hydrogen bonding is a specific type of dipole-dipole interaction that occurs when hydrogen is bonded to highly electronegative atoms like oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine. In the case of NH₃, where hydrogen is bonded to nitrogen, hydrogen bonding is indeed the dominant force. Correct

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding is a specific type of dipole-dipole interaction that occurs when hydrogen is bonded to highly electronegative atoms like oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine. In the case of NH₃, where hydrogen is bonded to nitrogen, hydrogen bonding is indeed the dominant force.

questions.text: Hydrogen bonding is maximum in:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Miscellaneous

options.explanation: Benzene does not exhibit hydrogen bonding because it is a nonpolar molecule. Benzene does not have any polar bonds, and it does not have any lone pairs of electrons.

Water exhibits hydrogen bonding because it is a polar molecule. Water has two polar bonds, and it has two lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atom. The hydrogen atoms in one water molecule can form hydrogen bonds with the lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atoms of other water molecules.

Ammonia exhibits hydrogen bonding because it is a polar molecule. Ammonia has one polar bond, and it has three lone pairs of electrons on the nitrogen atom. The hydrogen atoms in ammonia can form hydrogen bonds with the lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atoms of water molecules.

Hydrogen fluoride exhibits hydrogen bonding because it is a polar molecule. Hydrogen fluoride has one polar bond, and it has one lone pair of electrons on the fluorine atom. The hydrogen atom in hydrogen fluoride can form hydrogen bonds with the lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atoms of water molecules.

questions.explanation: Water exhibits hydrogen bonding because it is a polar molecule. Water has two polar bonds, and it has two lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atom. The hydrogen atoms in one water molecule can form hydrogen bonds with the lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atoms of other water molecules.

questions.text: Ice has_ structure.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Miscellaneous

options.explanation: Ice does not have a tetrahedral structure because all water molecules in ice are not equivalent. In a tetrahedral structure, each water molecule would be bonded to four other water molecules in a perfect tetrahedron.

An octahedral structure would have each water molecule bonded to 6 other water molecules. This is not possible because the oxygen atom in each water molecule only has 2 lone pairs of electrons. These lone pairs can only bond to 2 other water molecules.

Ice does not have a cubic structure because of the way that the water molecules are arranged. In a cubic structure, the water molecules would be arranged in a regular grid, with each molecule being bonded to four other molecules. The hexagonal lattice of ice is more stable than a cubic lattice because it allows the water molecules to form more hydrogen bonds. The more hydrogen bonds that are formed, the stronger the structure is.

The water molecules in ice are arranged in a hexagonal lattice, with each molecule being bonded to four other molecules. The hydrogen bonds between the water molecules are strong, which is why ice has a high melting point.

questions.explanation: For Fedral Board: The water molecules in ice are arranged in a hexagonal lattice, with each molecule being bonded to four other molecules. The hydrogen bonds between the water molecules are strong, which is why ice has a high melting point. For Punjab Board: The structure of ice is just like that of a diamond because each atom of carbon in diamond is at the center of tetrahedron just like the oxygen of water molecule in ice.

questions.text: Vapour Pressure does not depends on:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Miscellaneous

options.explanation: Vapour pressure is the pressure exerted by the vapour of a substance in equilibrium with its liquid or solid form.The vapour pressure of a substance depends on the temperature of the substance.As the temperature of a liquid or solid increases, the kinetic energy of the molecules increases.This means that more and more molecules have enough energy to escape from the liquid or solid and enter the gas phase.

Larger molecules have more surface area than smaller molecules. This means that there are more opportunities for intermolecular forces to form between the molecules, which makes it more difficult for them to escape into the gas phase. As a result, liquids with larger molecules have lower vapour pressures.

The strength of intermolecular forces determines how easily molecules can escape from the liquid phase into the gas phase. Stronger intermolecular forces hold molecules more tightly together, making it more difficult for them to escape. As a result, liquids with stronger intermolecular forces have lower vapour pressures.

Vapour pressure is a thermodynamic property of a liquid that measures the tendency of its molecules to escape into the gas phase. Vapour pressure is independent of the surface area of the liquid. The surface area of a liquid can affect the rate of evaporation, but it does not affect the vapour pressure.

questions.explanation: Vapour pressure is a thermodynamic property of a liquid that measures the tendency of its molecules to escape into the gas phase. The magnitude of vapour pressure does not depend upon the amount of liquid in the container or the volume of container. It also does not depend on surface area of a liquid. The surface area of a liquid can affect the rate of evaporation, but it does not affect the vapour pressure.

questions.text: The highest boiling point among the following is for:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: NH₃ is a polar molecule capable of forming hydrogen bonds due to the presence of a hydrogen atom bonded to a highly electronegative nitrogen atom. Hydrogen bonds are relatively strong intermolecular forces that elevate the boiling point of NH₃. Correct

Br₂ is a nonpolar molecule and primarily experiences London dispersion forces (van der Waals forces), which are weaker intermolecular forces compared to hydrogen bonding. Bromine's boiling point is relatively lower. Incorrect .

CHCl₃ is a polar molecule with dipole-dipole forces and London dispersion forces. While polar interactions are present, they are generally weaker than hydrogen bonding. Chloroform's boiling point is not as high as NH₃. Incorrect

Xe is a noble gas and exists as individual atoms with weak London dispersion forces. While London dispersion forces increase with increasing atomic or molecular size, they are generally weaker than other types of intermolecular forces. Noble gases have relatively low boiling points. Incorrect

questions.explanation: NH₃ is a polar molecule capable of forming hydrogen bonds due to the presence of a hydrogen atom bonded to a highly electronegative nitrogen atom. Hydrogen bonds are relatively strong intermolecular forces that elevate the boiling point of NH₃.

questions.text: Na and Mg crystallise in body-centered cubic and face-centered cubic crystals respectively, then the number of atoms of Na and Mg present in the unit cell of their respective crystals is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as the number of atoms of Na and Mg present in the unit cell of their respective crystals is 2 and 4 respectively.
This option is incorrect as the number of atoms of Na and Mg present in the unit cell of their respective crystals is 2 and 4 respectively.
This option is incorrect as the number of atoms of Na and Mg present in the unit cell of their respective crystals is 2 and 4 respectively.

The body centered cubic cell consistes of 8 atoms at the corners and one atom at the centre. ∴n = (8/8)+1= 2. The face centered cell consists of 8 atoms at the eight corners and one atom at each of the six faces. This atom at the faces is shared by two unit cells. ∴n = 8/8 + (6/2) = 4 Hence, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: The correct option is D (2 and 4 ) The body centered cubic cell consistes of 8 atoms at the corners and one atom at the centre. ∴n = (8/8)+1= 2. The face centered cell consists of 8 atoms at the eight corners and one atom at each of the six faces. This atom at the faces is shared by two unit cells. ∴n = 8/8 + (6/2) = 4

questions.text: For a real gas obeying van der waal’s equation, a graph is plotted between PVm (y-axis) and P(x axis) where Vm is molar volume. Find y-intercept of the graph.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: For a real gas obeying van der Waal’s equation, a graph is plotted between PVm (y-axis) and P (x-axis), where Vm is molar volume. The y-intercept of the graph is RT. This means that when the pressure of the gas is zero, the molar volume of the gas is equal to RT/P, where R is the gas constant and T is the temperature in Kelvin.

questions.text: The Kinetic Molecular Theory of gases predicts pressure to rise as the temperature of a gas increases because :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.text: The average kinetic energy of the gas molecules decreases
options.explanation: As temperature increases the average kinetic energy of gas molecules increases.

options.text: Gas molecules collide more frequently with the container walls
options.explanation: As the temperature increases, the average K.E of the molecules increases as K.E is directly proprtional to temperature. <K.E.> increses - Temperature increases Molecules move faster and collide with the walls of the container more frequently with more force. This increases the pressure exerted by the gas on the container.

options.text: Gas molecules collide less frequently with the container walls
options.explanation: Gas molecules move faster as their K.E increases, and collide with the walls of the container more frequently.

options.text: Gas molecules collide less energetically with the container walls
options.explanation: Kinetic Energy is directly proportional to the temperature of molecules. At higher temperatures, the particles move faster and collide more forcefully with the walls of the container.

questions.explanation: As the temperature increases, the average K.E of the molecules increases as K.E is directly proprtional to temperature. <K.E.> increses - Temperature increases Molecules move faster and collide with the walls of the container more frequently with more force. This increases the pressure exerted by the gas on the container.
